Why Choose Rezel Engine?

If you don't like to write a Blueprint or ColorfulBlock when you are writing an important Logic, want to write Logic with Code, this Engine is for you. Anyway, I just make the C++ to the best language for writing Game Logic, and I want to make a Engine that can write Game Logic with C++. So I make this Engine. However, I don't want to make a Engine that only can write Game Logic with C++. The Lua/C# Scripting will be support in future, This is a Simple Code to help you choice Rezel Engine:

#include <Rezel.h> class Sandbox : public engine::Application { public: engine::Scene* scene1; Sandbox() : engine::Application(1000, 700, 60), scene1(nullptr) { scene1 = new engine::Scene(); engine::Application::AddScene(scene1); } void Tick() override { Application::Tick(); } void Draw() override { Application::Draw(); } }; engine::Application* engine::CreateApplication() { return new Sandbox(); }

This a Basic Code when you are Creating an Application with Rezel Engine.(The C++ Version is C++20)

How Technology is Used in Rezel Engine?

  • Language:

    So In the Past I said that Rezel Engine use C++20 as the Main Language, Lua&C# will be in future.

  • Renderer(Include UI&2D):

    1. OpenGL:

      If you use the low version, Such as Rezel Engine 1.1.8, the Renderer is only OpenGL3.3, Of Course, It will easy to draw.

    2. Vulkan:

      If you use the high version, the Renderer is Vulkan&OpenGL. You can choose the Renderer you want.

    3. ImGui:

      The UI is made by ImGui.

  • Physics:

    Powerd By Box2D

  • Main Use:

    Actually, Window&other thing are Powerd By SDL3

Get Started

Download Editor or Sanbox, then if you need help, you can read the Docx.
